meaning: abortion, aborted foetus, stunted, shoots, cost, expenses, troublesome matter, problem; dāʾdǝʾa (MA,M), sāʾdǝʾa (T) 'abort, miscarry, bear expenses, remain uncompleted, be little' (denominative); tadäʾdǝʾa, tasäʾdǝʾa, (T) tadäʾadäʾa 'remain uncompleted, be little, (T) be impure'; ʾadäʾdǝʾa, ʾasäʾdǝʾa 'hire' (lit. 'cause to bear expenses');
origin_explanation: däʾdäʾ with the meanings stated above comes from the root wḍʾ 'go out' (see below). This root explains the meanings 'aborted foetus, expenses'; cp. Heb. ṣeʾĕṣāʾīm 'offspring, descendants', likewise from the root yṣʾ 'go out'. Ar. duʾduʾ, diʾdiʾ 'many descendants' even though there is no Arabic root wdʾ meaning 'go out'. Praetorius 1879:94 connects G. däʾdäʾ with Amh. č̣uč̣ut 'young of chicken'; however, č̣uč̣ut probably derives from an onomatopoetic expression č̣č̣. For the connection between däʾdäʾ 'problem' and tadäʾdäʾa 'remain uncompleted, be incomplete', cp. Amh. gudday 'problem, matter', from gʷäddälä 'be wanting', that is, 'something that is wanting, that is to be done, matter, affair'. The meaning 'be little, become little' could be derived from 'bear the expenses > spend > become little'. For the meaning 'troublesome matter', cp. Amh. fäṭa.
amharic_translation: ውርጃ፣ የተጨናገፈ ፅንስ፣ የደረቀ፣ ቡቃያ፣ ወጪ፣ ችግር ያለበት ጉዳይ፣ ችግር፤ dāʾdǝʾa (MA,M), sāʾdǝʾa (T) 'ማቋረጥ፣ ማስወረድ፣ ወጪ መሸከም፣ ሳይጠናቀቅ መቅረት፣ ትንሽ መሆን' (ስም); tadäʾdǝʾa, tasäʾdǝʾa, (T) tadäʾadäʾa 'ሳይጠናቀቅ መቅረት፣ ትንሽ መሆን፣ (T) ንፁህ አለመሆን'; ʾadäʾdǝʾa, ʾasäʾdǝʾa 'መቅጠር' (በትርጉሙ 'ወጪ እንዲሸከም ማድረግ');

origin_explanation: SEMITIC: Ar. dayʿa 'village, countryside, field', for which a Geez origin was suggested by Noeldeke 1910:59.
amharic_translation: ዝቅተኛ ቦታዎች፣ ሸለቆ፣ ሜዳ፣ የግጦሽ መሬት

meaning: thunderstorm, crash of thunder, thunderbolt, thundering, lightning, (KG) glittering, sparkling
origin_explanation: ETHIOPIC: Gur. tat, tʾat 'thunder, lightning', Amh. ta alä 'make noise, crack', tat alä 'crackle'. Tna. taʿtaʿ bälä 'crackle', Te. čäčäʿ 'crash'; also in Cushitic: Bed. tēw 'crash of thunder', and in Moča fēyo 'crackle'.
amharic_translation: ነጎድጓድ፣ የነጎድጓድ ድምፅ፣ መብረቅ፣ ነጎድጓድ፣ መብረቅ፣ (KG) የሚያብረቀርቅ፣ የሚያንፀባርቅ

origin_explanation: SEMITIC: Ar. ḍabuʿ, ḍabʿ 'hyena', Soq. ḍabwəh, Heb. ṣābūʿ, PHH. ṣābōʿ 'hyena, leopard', Aram.-Syr. ʾaḇʿā 'hyena' (Brockelmann 1908:136). Von Soden 143 (followed by Fronzaroli 1969, V, 23) also compares Akk. būṣu 'hyena'. For the origin of Heb. ṣābūʿ, see Buhl 672. See also ṣ-b.
see tabdan, tabnada.

meaning: handle, ring attached to a box or to another utensil
amharic_translation: እጀታ፣ ከሳጥን ወይም ከሌላ ዕቃ ጋር የተያያዘ ቀለበት

origin_explanation: if there is a lack of precision, dabart may then be connected with ṣäbart 'palm' (for which see below).

meaning: be slack, become withered, be limp, become weak, become infirm, be powerless, become paralytic, be intimidated
amharic_translation: መላላት፣ መድረቅ፣ መዝለፍለፍ፣ መዳከም፣ መታመም፣ ኃይል ማጣት፣ ሽባ መሆን፣ መፍራት

meaning: weaking, sickly, cowardly, one with withered members, lame
origin_explanation: B 90 Zəhab kəfəl was wrongly copied for zəhab kəfəl.
amharic_translation: የደከመ፣ የታመመ፣ ፈሪ፣ የደረቁ እግሮች ያሉት፣ አንካሳ

meaning: verbal noun of däbṭa; also 'infirmity, weakness'
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: Ar. ḍabṭ 'weak'. Barth 1903 b compares Heb. ʾābhā 'seizure of feebleness' (with metathesis). See also dəwaṣa.
amharic_translation: የ däbṭa ግስ ስም፤ እንዲሁም 'ድካም፣ ድክመት'

meaning: grasp, take hold of, lay hands on
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: Ar. ḍabaṭa 'seize'. Ms. dəybeṭ, Heb. ṣābaṭ 'seize' is perhaps a loanword. Akk. ṣabātu 'seize' is perhaps also Md. ṣbṭ, also associate with < Akk. ṣabātu (according to Dterich 302). A related form also occurs in Heb. ʾeby 'bundle of ears of corn', PHH ṣebet 'node' (for Aram.-Syr. ṣbṭ, see Justi 1875, 43, n. 1, Greenfield, JAOS 82 (1962) 292).
ETHIOPIC: Te. ṣäbḥa 'seize', Amh. č̣äbbäṭä, Gaf. č̣äbbäṭä, Arg. č̣ebäṭä 'squeeze', Gur. č̣äbbäṭä, č̣äbäṭä 'seize, hold'. See also däbṭ.
ETHIOPIC: Tna. dəbṭi 'gonorrhea', Amh. č̣äbṭ, č̣äbṭ from the root č̣bṭ of something that seizes one (see däbṭäḥa, above), cp. also Te. čaʾəbṭay 'be smitten by calamity'.

SEMITIC: Buhl 541 (followed by Koehler 662, Baumgartner 718, von Soden 1032) compares Ar. sūs 'moth-worm', Heb. sās, Aram.-Syr. sāsā, Md. sus, Akk. sāsu. The identification D (Geez) s (Semitic) is not convincing.
Te. ṣəf, Amh. ğəğəw 'swarm of ants', č̣əč̣əw 'kind of a small ant'.
SEMITIC: Ar. dafʿ 'excrement', Mh. dōfəʿ 'cow dung', Heb. ṣōpāʿ 'dung'.
ETHIOPIC: Te. däfnä 'bury', Tna. däfänä, Amh. däffänä, probably passed into Cushitic: Bil. dāf 'excrement'.

meaning: braid, plait, drape, weave, interwine, be linked to one another, plot, intrigue, subvert
amharic_translation: መጎንጎን፣ መጠለፍ፣ መሸፈን፣ መሸመን፣ መተሳሰር፣ እርስ በርስ መያያዝ፣ ማሴር፣ ሴራ መሸረብ፣ ማፍረስ

origin_explanation: SEMITIC: Tna. ṣəfər 'braid', Te. ṣäfər 'braid' (Praetorius 1871:127), Amh. təfər 'braid' is woven (leather strap). Amh. hat təfər, dəfər 'braid' is probably borrowed from a Sem. language. See also däfərt.
probably from däfara 'intertwine', the tapeworms being intertwined to one another or to the interior of the stomach.

meaning: make up a bed, lean upon, be close to
origin_explanation: the time of the miracle of Christ with the paralytic).

meaning: lie on one's side, recline, couch, bed
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: Te. dägʿa 'lie on one's side, recline', Tna. dägʿe 'lie down', mədgəʿ 'seat, support', dəngwəʿ 'lie on the side', mədgəʿ 'place for reclining, seat, crib'. The meanings 'lean upon, rest against, recline, place to lean upon, rest' > 'be paralytic' (denominative from mədgəʿ 'place for reclining') get near, take shelter under, ʾədgəʿ 'lean one thing against the other', mədgəʿ 'cushion decorated with silk and gold braids'. See also dängwəʿ, and təgəʿa 1.

meaning: be on the left hand, be left-handed
origin_explanation: H 223) be distorted, be left, go left.
amharic_translation: በግራ በኩል መሆን፣ ግራ እጅ መሆን

origin_explanation: (D) from left side, left hand.

meaning: being twisted, being on the left side

origin_explanation: SEMITIC: Ar. dgm 'be distorted, be crooked' (Dillmann 1336). W. Müller suggests, with reservations, G. dgwam speak out of the side of the mouth.

ETHIOPIC: Tna. tədägäʿe 'left (dägam 'left-handed')', perhaps also Tna. tədägäʿe 'be inconvenienced, disturbed', Te. dägäʿ 'lie down', dägämay 'sinister, evil'. The root is possibly common with Cushitic: Awngi. təgəh 'left' (with alternation h:m), Bil. qua-lungah, with inserted n, hence Te. Tna. tədägäʿ 'left', Aw. təgəh 'left'.

meaning: beat the ground with the feet (one's feet and legs), move about on the feet, pace up and down, move about, dance, clap the hands in time to the music
amharic_translation: እግርን መሬት ላይ መምታት (እግሩንና እግሩን)፣ በእግር መዞር፣ ወደ ላይና ወደ ታች መሄድ፣ መዞር፣ መደነስ፣ ከሙዚቃው ጋር እጅ ማጨብጨብ

origin_explanation: SEMITIC: related to Ar. daḥasa 'throw about (the feet), dahasa 'agitate the feet', daḥdaḥa 'jerk' (slaughtered animal)'

meaning: stamp on a sown field, figuratively in rabbinical language 'overtake, break through, violate (the law), Amh. dms 'crash through (an obstacle), break through, violate (agreements), Gur. zms 'violate (the law)'.
origin_explanation: ETHIOPIC: Tna. ṣəbəṭ 'stamp on a sown field'.
amharic_translation: በተዘራ እርሻ ላይ መርገጥ፣ በምሳሌያዊ አነጋገር በረቢ ቋንቋ 'ማለፍ፣ መስበር፣ መጣስ (ሕግን)፣ አምሃ. ድምስ 'እንቅፋትን መስበር፣ መስበር፣ መጣስ (ስምምነቶችን)፣ ጉራ. ዝምስ 'ሕግን መጣስ'።

origin_explanation: related to tənsaʾe; see above.

SEMITIC: Ar. ḍḥw 'sun', Heb. ṣaḥ (so Eitan, HUCA 12-13, 1937-8, p. 61; in connection with ṣḥy, ṣḥḥ, for which see ṣäḥwa, above. Note that Ar. ḍḥw 'sun' is formed as Reinwortbildung with ḥw 'air' as second element (Brockelmann Gr. 927:14-16).

meaning: be like the sun, warm oneself (in the sun)
origin_explanation: ETHIOPIC: Te. Tna. ṣäḥay 'sun', Gaf. č̣äy, č̣e (literary language), Arg. č̣äḥay, Gur. č̣e. See also dḥy.

meaning: be like the sun, warm oneself (in the sun)
origin_explanation: denominative of däḥay (see above).

meaning: hip, hipbone, thigh, thighbone, flesh of thigh, shoulder blade
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: The connection with Ar. šāq 'thigh', Heb. šōq, Syr. šāqā suggested by Brockelmann 1908:239 is unlikely.
ethiopic_script: ፀኲ፣ ፃኲ፣ ጸኲ፣ ጸክ፣ ጾክ፣ ፀኮ
amharic_translation: ዳሌ፣ የዳሌ አጥንት፣ ጭን፣ የጭን አጥንት፣ የጭን ሥጋ፣ የትከሻ አጥንት

origin_explanation: from dgm 'left', above. It is an analogical formation with ymn 'right', above.

meaning: listen, hearken, heed, incline (the ear), give ear
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: related to syʿ/šmʿ (from swʿ) 'overhear, listen in silence'. Amh. č̣əmäč̣əmä 'rumor' (that is, what one hears).
amharic_translation: ማዳመጥ፣ መስማት፣ ትኩረት መስጠት፣ ጆሮ መስጠት

meaning: harness, bind together, join, link, subjugate
amharic_translation: ማሰር፣ አንድ ላይ ማሰር፣ መቀላቀል፣ ማገናኘት፣ ማስገዛት

meaning: yoke oneself, be yoked, bind oneself, join oneself, be an adherent of, adhere, be a disciple, subjugate oneself, be associated, be in attendance, dedicate oneself, devote oneself to, serve, be subject, be a slave, be addicted, serve, serve in a devout manner, be devoted to something assiduously
origin_explanation: passed into Cushitic: Bil. č̣əmədən 'reckon, surround'. For the meaning 'dry', see also ṣəmṣəma.
amharic_translation: ራስን ማሰር፣ መታሰር፣ ራስን ማሰር፣ ራስን መቀላቀል፣ የአንድ ነገር ተከታይ መሆን፣ መከተል፣ ደቀ መዝሙር መሆን፣ ራስን ማስገዛት፣ መያያዝ፣ መገኘት፣ ራስን መስጠት፣ ራስን ለአንድ ነገር መስጠት፣ ማገልገል፣ ተገዢ መሆን፣ ባሪያ መሆን፣ ሱስ መያዝ፣ ማገልገል፣ በአክብሮት ማገልገል፣ ለአንድ ነገር በትጋት መሰጠት

meaning: unite, join, affix, connect, attach, fasten together, nail down, impale together
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: Heb. ṣmd 'join', Aram.-Syr. ṣmd 'bind so-gether, yoke', Ar. ḍmd 'bind, join', Ug. ṣmd 'join, pair, yoke', Jib. ṣmd 'bind', Arg. č̣ämäda, Gur. č̣ämäda; passed into Cushitic: Qab. č̣imad 'yoke', Qab. č̣im-id 'pair of oxen', Aw. č̣əmad 'a yoke'. Or. č̣imada.
amharic_translation: ማዋሃድ፣ መቀላቀል፣ ማያያዝ፣ ማገናኘት፣ ማያያዝ፣ አንድ ላይ መቸንከር፣ አንድ ላይ መወጋት

meaning: bind, tie around, patch up, restrain, tie up, heal, strengthen, cover, dry it, treat a fracture, strengthen
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: Ar. ḍamana 'bring together', join, enclose, surround', PHH ṣimṣem (from ṣmṣm) 'press, oppress'. For 'hard', cp. also Ar. ṣawmaʿ (from ṣwm) 'hard'.
amharic_translation: ማሰር፣ ማሰር፣ መጠገን፣ መገደብ፣ ማሰር፣ ማዳን፣ ማጠናከር፣ መሸፈን፣ ማድረቅ፣ ስብራትን ማከም፣ ማጠናከር

origin_explanation: ETHIOPIC: Te. č̣ämäʿa 'squeeze together', Amh. č̣ämäqqä 'bind, tie with a'.

ETHIOPIC: Te. tämara, Tna. tämärä, Amh. ṭämmärä, Arg. č̣emärä, Gaf. č̣ämmärä, Gur. č̣ämmärä. A connection with mḥr (t, and (d)mḥr) with t seems to be borrowed from Amharic. Tigrinya təmbərhā, təbərhā 'mix', augmented by n (becoming m before b) is to be re-examined. W. Müller rather connects with a root with augmented r, suggested in Wright 1937b:18; this is because an augmentation with h is more normal than an augmentation with b. The root passed into Cushitic: Bil. dämmar 'tie, gather'.
SEMITIC: Heb. ṣemer 'wool', Aram.-Syr. ʿmr, Md. ʿamra, Syr. ʿmrā 'wool'.
SEMITIC: Ug. ṣmd (with alternance t:r), see above, Borer, Lg 23 (1968) 132.
ETHIOPIC: Tna. ṣəmd 'wood', Amh. ṣəmd 'white cloth made of a mixture of silk and wool' (from Geez); also in Cushitic: Kham. səmər 'hair, hairy'. Aw. ṣəmər, Bil. šəmər.

meaning: be quiet, not emit a sound, be lonely, be separated from others, lead a quiet life
amharic_translation: ዝም ማለት፣ ድምፅ አለማሰማት፣ ብቸኛ መሆን፣ ከሌሎች መለየት፣ ጸጥ ያለ ሕይወት መምራት

meaning: quietly, silently, peacefully, carefully, in a low voice
amharic_translation: በጸጥታ፣ በዝምታ፣ በሰላም፣ በጥንቃቄ፣ በዝቅተኛ ድምፅ

meaning: stillness, tranquility, quietness, loneliness, solitude, solitary life, contemplative life, contemplation, life of asceticism, sedateness
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: Tna. təmbəlo 'be lonely, be solitary', Amh. č̣əm alä 'become quiet, become silent', təbəlo 'alone, by oneself', č̣əm 'in a quiet way' (being alone, from Geez). The connection with Te. č̣əmlə 'be lonely' suggested by Littmann-Höfner 624 is unlikely. See for this root Praetorius 1890:35, no. 27.
amharic_translation: መረጋጋት፣ ሰላም፣ ጸጥታ፣ ብቸኝነት፣ ብቸኝነት፣ ብቸኛ ሕይወት፣ አሳቢ ሕይወት፣ ማሰላሰል፣ የ asceticism ሕይወት፣ መረጋጋት

meaning: is also the name of a person and the name of a city
amharic_translation: የሰው ስም እና የከተማ ስም ነው።

meaning: ornament for the forehead, diadem, black silk
origin_explanation: ETHIOPIC: Te. ṣəmbəl, ṣəmbəli 'frontal', Amh. ğənbəl, ğəbəla 'headcloth'.
note that m:f of dmd became n:f in dnf.

from dəfära 'braid, weave, intertwine' (see above) with inserted n, but it is also possible to derive it from a kind of creeping plant used to make a snare (see below) with the development of meaning, cp. Amh. əṭkəlla (translation of sənfər) 'kind of creeping rty', and trap that is made of the əṭkəlla). The connection with Amh. wəṭəmmər 'trap for catching mice or birds' suggested by Praetorius 1879:116 is unlikely.

meaning: lie on the couch, recline on a cushion
origin_explanation: Source: the connection with Heb. ṣlʿ 'lie on the side', from rln 1878:404 is unlikely.
amharic_translation: በሶፋ ላይ መተኛት፣ ትራስ ላይ መደገፍ

meaning: lie on the side, recline on cushions, place where one reposes, wall to lean against, stable, stall, crib, pen
origin_explanation: ETHIOPIC: Te. č̣əläʿe 'lie on the side', from č̣lʿ suggested by syʿ, see dägʿa, above.
amharic_translation: በጎን መተኛት፣ ትራስ ላይ መደገፍ፣ ማረፊያ ቦታ፣ ለመደገፍ ግድግዳ፣ በረት፣ በረት፣ ጋጣ፣ እስክርቢቶ

origin_explanation: perhaps connected with mə-dägəʿ 'paralytic', the disease being identified with intense heat.
ETHIOPIC: Tna. təbəla-ṣe 'she conceived', Amh. ṭənäsä 'conceive', Gur. ṭənäsä.

meaning: wedge, hooks to make a hedge, wall of a house
amharic_translation: ሽብልቅ፣ አጥር ለመሥራት መንጠቆዎች፣ የቤት ግድግዳ

meaning: hedge, fence, enclosure, wall, fortification, sticks used for making a wall
amharic_translation: አጥር፣ አጥር፣ አጥር፣ ግድግዳ፣ ምሽግ፣ ግድግዳ ለመሥራት የሚያገለግሉ እንጨቶች

origin_explanation: (Gr.443) guinea hen

meaning: be annulled, be made of no effect, be idle
amharic_translation: መሰረዝ፣ ውጤት አልባ መሆን፣ ስራ ፈት መሆን

meaning: bring to a stop, make stop, neglect, cease, make cease, make worthless, be idle, leave idle, abandon, give up, interrupt, abrogate, omit, keep back, hinder, divert, leave aside, abolish, make void, use up; also causative of the meanings of däräb
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: Barth 1902:42 connects it with Ar. daraba 'he which he hindered, he set (false before the truth), he turned'. G. (däräb 'useless') is interpreted as 'coming to an end', but in fact it means 'draw near, approach' and not 'come to an end'.
amharic_translation: ማቆም፣ ማቆም፣ ችላ ማለት፣ ማቆም፣ ማቆም፣ ከንቱ ማድረግ፣ ስራ ፈት መሆን፣ ስራ ፈት መተው፣ መተው፣ ማቆም፣ ማቋረጥ፣ መሰረዝ፣ መተው፣ መከልከል፣ ማዘናጋት፣ ወደ ጎን መተው፣ ማጥፋት፣ ባዶ ማድረግ፣ መጠቀም፤ እንዲሁም የ däräb ትርጉሞች መንስኤ

origin_explanation: ETHIOPIC: Tna. drb 'back', embryo.
ETHIOPIC: Tna. č̣ərqi 'rag', Te. Amh. Arg. Gaf. Gur. č̣ärq, into Cushitic: Kam. č̣ərqu, Qab. č̣ərqa, Bil. č̣ərq.

meaning: turn about, be troubled, be restless, be tormented, be afflicted
origin_explanation: ETHIOPIC: Te. ənsərora, ənsərora 'be dizzy', Tna. ənsərora 'produce dizziness', zrwr bälä 'feel dizzy'.
amharic_translation: መዞር፣ መጨነቅ፣ እረፍት ማጣት፣ መሰቃየት፣ መታመም

meaning: become an enemy, become a foe, be hostile, be an adversary, rebel, revolt, stir up trouble, start a fight, start a war
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: Ar. ḍarra 'be harmful', SAr. ḍrr 'show wage war', Soq. ḍer(r) 'strike', Heb. ṣārar 'show hostility toward, ṣar 'enemy', Aram. ʿrr 'enemy', Ug. ʿrr 'hurt, affect', ʿr 'enemy', Md. ʿara, Jib. ʿer 'enemy, enmity', also 'rival wife' (< Ar. ḍarra, Heb. ṣar 'rival wife'), cp. also Akk. ṣerru.
amharic_translation: ጠላት መሆን፣ ጠላት መሆን፣ ጠላት መሆን፣ ተቃዋሚ መሆን፣ ማመፅ፣ ማመፅ፣ ችግር መፍጠር፣ ጠብ መጀመር፣ ጦርነት መጀመር

origin_explanation: ETHIOPIC: Tna. ṣär 'enemy', Te. ṣär, Amh. ṭälat, Arg. ṭälat, Gaf. ṭälata, Gur. ṭälat, Har. ṭäy, also in Cushitic: Bil. ṣär 'enemy'.
metathesis of dämara 'unite, join'.
SEMITIC: Ar. ḍirs 'tooth', ḍarisa 'become dull (tooth)', SAr. ḍrs 'molar tooth'. Bittner 1909:30 compares Mh. ḥarśēt 'molar tooth' (with ḥ:ḍ).

meaning: be set on edge (teeth), become dull (teeth), break off the edge of the teeth
origin_explanation: denominative of dərs;
amharic_translation: ጥርሶች መሳል፣ ጥርሶች መደብዘዝ፣ የጥርሶችን ጫፍ መስበር

origin_explanation: ETHIOPIC: Tna. tə(ʾa)daraṣä 'have an angry look, be sullen, be gloomy', Te. (ʾa)daraṣä 'look gloomy, distort one's face', (ʾa)dawaṣä 'be surly'.
ETHIOPIC: Tna. č̣äwa 'freedborn'. Brockelmann 1950 b derives from Bil. č̣ä 'family' (probably 'a good family > freeborn').

meaning: be malignant, be perverse, be vicious, be cruel, be ferocious, be fierce, be savage
amharic_translation: ክፉ መሆን፣ ጠማማ መሆን፣ ጨካኝ መሆን፣ ጨካኝ መሆን፣ ጨካኝ መሆን፣ ጨካኝ መሆን፣ አረመኔ መሆን

meaning: be weakened, be crippled, be lamed, be paralyzed, be twisted
amharic_translation: መዳከም፣ ሽባ መሆን፣ አንካሳ መሆን፣ ሽባ መሆን፣ መጣመም

meaning: weakened, feeble, twisted, drooping, maimed, paralyzed, crippled, one palsied, defective
origin_explanation: is a variant of dəbṭa (see above). For 'castrate', cp. Ar. ḥawwala, from which Te. ḥəwla is borrowed.
amharic_translation: የደከመ፣ ደካማ፣ የተጣመመ፣ የደረቀ፣ የተጎዳ፣ ሽባ፣ አንካሳ፣ ሽባ የሆነ፣ ጉድለት ያለበት

meaning: take prisoner, capture, carry captives, deport, exile, seize (goods)
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: Te. səwa 'take prisoner, steal', Tna. č̣äwä 'kidnap to sell as slaves'. Brockelmann, ZS 8 (1932) 116-7 compares it with Ar. dawwa 'be lean, scrawny, weak'. Egyptian dw 'bad', for the meaning 'weak' take prisoner, but dw 'bad' is a late coptic adition. This comparison is not convincing.
amharic_translation: ማሰር፣ መያዝ፣ ምርኮኞችን መውሰድ፣ ማባረር፣ ማባረር፣ መያዝ (ዕቃዎች)
